items.description: after the up() migration runs, the stock table has a batch_number column
after the up() migration runs, the stock table has a serial_number column
the Alembic revision module does not import app/routes or app/services; persistence logic stays in the repository/migration layer
the app and Alembic env read DB_NAME/PGDATABASE from the environment; no hardcoded DSN exists anywhere in the migration or config path
after up() then down(), the original inventory_code value is reconstructed for every row and the batch_number/serial_number columns are removed
the integration test suite connects to the real Lakebase branch DB (DB_NAME=stockflow) and not an in-memory substitute; the conftest fixture verifies the live connection before any scenario runs
a stock row with inventory_code 'A12-B7-S001' has batch_number='B7' and serial_number='S001' after the backfill
a stock row with inventory_code 'A12-B7' has batch_number='B7' and serial_number=NULL after the backfill
a stock row with inventory_code 'A12' has batch_number=NULL and serial_number=NULL after the backfill
every row in a 100-row stock table has batch_number and serial_number present (populated or NULL) after the migration, with no rows skipped
the location column value is unchanged for a row after the migration runs
the UNIQUE(sku, location) constraint exists on the stock table after the migration and rejects a duplicate (sku, location) pair
sku and quantity are unchanged for a row after the migration runs
quantity values are identical for every row before and after the migration; no negative quantity is introduced
